channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Dark Matter May Be Made of Black Holes From Another Universe - WIRED
item.description:
1. Dark Matter May Be Made of Black Holes From Another Universe  WIRED
  2. Exploding black holes could explain an antimatter mystery  Science News
  3. Ripples in spacetime may have revealed 1st evidence of tiny black holes born in the Big Bang  Space
  4. Scientists unearth new theory about how the universe began – and it’s not just the Big Bang  The Independent
  5. Relic black holes from cosmic ‘bounce’ may be dark matter shaping our Universe  Interesting Engineering

item.title: Laser-plasma accelerator drives free-electron laser for record 8 hours - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Laser-plasma accelerator drives free-electron laser for record 8 hours  Phys.org
  2. TAU Systems Demonstrates Stable Laser-Driven Free-Electron Laser Operation for Over Eight Hours  Unite.AI
  3. Laser-driven free electron laser runs for more than eight hours  Physics World

item.title: Baby Neanderthals may have had a rapid growth spurt compared to modern babies - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Baby Neanderthals may have had a rapid growth spurt compared to modern babies  Phys.org
  2. Neanderthal infants were enormous compared with modern humans  New Scientist
  3. Amud 7, the Neanderthal baby who shows they developed faster than modern humans  EL PAÍS English
  4. Neanderthal Newborns Were Absolute Units. Here’s Exactly How Big.  VICE
  5. Massive Neanderthal Baby Shows How Different They Were To Modern Human Infants  IFLScience

item.title: Stephen Hawking's black hole information paradox could be solved — if the universe has 7 dimensions - Live Science
item.description: Stephen Hawking's black hole information paradox could be solved — if the universe has 7 dimensions  Live Science

item.title: 9,000-Year-Old Structures Deep Beneath the Great Lakes Reveal Archaeological Evidence of an Ancient “Lost World” - The Debrief
item.description: 9,000-Year-Old Structures Deep Beneath the Great Lakes Reveal Archaeological Evidence of an Ancient “Lost World”  The Debrief

item.title: Ancient process that created rare earth elements discovered — and it could help us locate desperately needed deposits - Live Science
item.description:
1. Ancient process that created rare earth elements discovered — and it could help us locate desperately needed deposits  Live Science
  2. The Geological Past Behind Today’s Clean Energy Materials  The Economic Times
  3. Tectonic map reveals most rare earth elements formed in ancient subduction zones  Earth.com
  4. Ancient tectonic zones hold key to locating rare earth elements: study  El.kz
  5. Ancient tectonic processes are the key to locating rare minerals  Phys.org
